summary: Merged decomp PR for the grYorster stage module. It replaced stubs/unknown signatures with C for several Yorster callbacks, fully matched grYorster_802024F0, improved grYorster_802022A4 and grYorster_8020266C to high partial matches, and expanded the stage-specific parameter and track-element layouts needed by those functions.

key_files.role: Main implementation changes for grYorster_802022A4, grYorster_80202428, grYorster_802024F0, and grYorster_8020266C; added dependencies on grlib, grmaterial, item, lb, and JObj APIs.
Replaced grYorster_802024F0 UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with a typed collision callback prototype and added mp forward declarations.
Expanded grYorster_TrackElement from mostly padding into offset-preserving fields x00, x01, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C.

what_changed: Expanded YorsterParams from padding plus x14 into typed fields x00, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C, and added pad_04 to grYt_804D6A20_t.

Implemented grYorster_802022A4 to initialize nine track elements using joints 0xA through 0x12, store each element's JObj and material GObj, register grYorster_80202428 as a material callback, register grYorster_802024F0 through mpJointSetCb2, and start ground animation state 1.
Improved grYorster_80202428 so material interactions accumulate value into the matching track element's x04 field, while retaining the existing fighter check and ftLib_80086A4C call.

Replaced the grYorster_802024F0 placeholder with a typed collision callback taking Ground, joint id, CollData, an unused s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32 value. The function filters CollData env flags, handles fighter and item cases via ftLib and it_8026B718 calls, and accumulates collision value into the matching element's x08 field.

Implemented grYorster_8020266C as a per-element state machine using track-element counters, material enable/disable calls, grAnime_801C7FF8, mpLib_80057BC0, mpJointListAdd, lbGetJObjEndFrame, HSD_JObjGetTranslation, grLib_801C9EE8, and Ground_801C53EC.
Updated gryorster.h to include mp/forward.h and expose the concrete grYorster_802024F0 signature instead of UNK_PARAMS.
Updated gr/types.h with a concrete grYorster_TrackElement layout that preserves offsets while exposing the JObj pointer at x18 and material GObj pointer at x1C.
smart_moves: Tied together initialization, material callback accumulation, collision callback accumulation, and the per-frame updater through the same nine-element grYorster_TrackElement array, which makes the stage behavior traceable across callbacks.
Used an explicit joint-id table for joints 0xA through 0x12 in grYorster_802022A4, then derived map joints with Ground_801C32D4 at callback/update sites instead of hard-coding the derived values.
Converted grYorster_802024F0 from an unknown prototype to a concrete callback signature, allowing CollData fields and mpLib_GroundEnum to be represented in headers.
Kept uncertain struct members as offset-style xNN names and explicit padding rather than over-naming fields with weak evidence.
Preserved match-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK(8), casts, exact float constants, and state values while still making the control flow readable.
The decomp.dev report shows the work produced 4 new matches, including grYorster_802024F0 and .sdata/.data/.sbss for the gryorster unit.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules with callback-heavy behavior, recover the shared ground-vars struct first enough to connect initialization, collision callbacks, material callbacks, and update loops.

When a callback is registered through an engine function such as mpJointSetCb2, its call site can justify replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with a real prototype and adding only the needed forward declarations to the header.
Offset-style names like x04, x08, x0C, and x10 are acceptable when semantics are not yet proven; preserving layout is more important than speculative names.
Data-section matching can improve from small struct and global layout fixes, not only from function bodies; this PR's bot report showed .data, .sdata, and .sbss gains.
Material and collision interactions may need separate accumulators: this PR uses x04 for material callback accumulation and x08 for collision callback accumulation before the updater consumes them.
Exact stack padding, casts, comma-expression oddities, and floating-point constants can be necessary in partially matched functions and should be retained when they are present in the matching diff.
naming_conventions: Stage functions keep address-based names such as grYorster_802022A4, grYorster_802024F0, and grYorster_8020266C.
Yorster globals use the grYt_ prefix, as in grYt_803E5198, grYt_803E51CC, and grYt_804D6A20.
The stage-specific track struct is named grYorster_TrackElement, while uncertain members remain offset-named x00, x01, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C.
Headers use forward includes for referenced types, shown by adding mp/forward.h for mpLib_GroundEnum.
Local names mix semantic names and matching-style names, such as gp, gobj, coll_data, joint_id, entry, and temp_r28.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: grYorster_802024F0 includes PAD_STACK(8), indicating stack layout was match-sensitive.
The code preserves the unusual ftLib_800873F4((0, coll_data->x0_gobj)) expression seen in the diff, likely to match generated code shape.
grYorster_802022A4 uses a local s32 joints[9] initializer with values 0xA through 0x12, matching the looped initialization pattern.
The update state machine uses explicit state values 2, 3, and 4 plus a default case, preserving observed control-flow shape rather than abstracting it away.
Frame calculations retain casts to s32 and f32 and exact constants such as 10.0f, 0.3f, 1.0f, 0x143, and 0x19.
The grYorster_80202428 cast removal from ftLib_80086A4C's second argument is documented in the diff as a tiny matching cleanup.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviewer feedback were captured in this slice.
The only captured comment was the decomp.dev report for GALE01, which reported matched code at 65.02% (+380 bytes) and matched data at 38.57% (+200 bytes).
The bot report listed 4 new matches: grYorster_802024F0 at 100.00%, .sdata at 100.00%, .data at 100.00%, and .sbss at 100.00% for main/melee/gr/gryorster.
The bot report listed unmatched-item improvements: grYorster_8020266C to 86.66%, grYorster_802022A4 to 87.45%, .rodata to 94.74%, .sdata2 to 83.33%, and grYorster_80202428 to 99.40%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching grYorster_802022A4 or grYorster_8020266C after this PR's 87.45% and 86.66% partials.
Search for mpJointSetCb2 callback signatures in other stage modules to validate grYorster_802024F0's typed prototype.
Look for later renames of grYorster_TrackElement fields x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C once semantics are better established.
Compare other grMaterial_801C8CFC callback users to understand the material-hit accumulation pattern used by grYorster_80202428.
Search for OSReport("*** End Frame = %d\n") in grYorster-related work to see whether it remains intentional or is later cleaned up.
